reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — Node inference across six languages, headless first-visit + warm-cache browser runs, desktop + mobile screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Real onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-finetuned-squad2-ONNX question-answering runs in models/multilingual-qa/worker.js. Headless first-visit browser run (WASM, no GPU): clicked Download (~295 MB) → state 'ready' → extracted '330 metres tall' for the default English sample. Standalone Node run over six languages produced correctly-located spans: EN 'How tall…' → '330 metres'; DE 'Wie hoch…' → '330 Meter'; FR 'Où se trouve…' → 'Paris'; ES → '1889'; JA '東京タワーの高さ…' → '333メートル'; IT → 'Parigi'. 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Genuinely multilingual: one XLM-R model extracts answer spans across Latin, CJK and (via dir=auto) RTL scripts, no translation step and no per-language model.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches the QA contract: two distributions over the context tokens (start/end logits), read straight off pipe.model. worker.js picks the best span, decodes it, then RE-LOCATES it in the original passage with a whitespace-insensitive scan — because the stock QA pipeline drops inter-word spaces for SentencePiece (XLM-R) it would return '330metres'; the demo instead shows the correctly-spaced '330 metres tall' AND highlights the exact char range. 'See inside' renders the real softmaxed start/end token strip with the ▁ word-boundary marker stripped.
rubric.notes: The whitespace-insensitive locate() is the key correctness fix; verified in-browser the highlighted answer keeps the passage's real spacing/casing.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance: model id onnx-community/xlm-roberta-base-finetuned-squad2-ONNX linked to card, task question-answering, architecture XLM-RoBERTa base (100 languages) SQuAD 2.0, quantization q8 (8-bit) ONNX, backend WebAssembly, download ~295 MB (with an explicit note that this is the heaviest QA demo due to the 250k multilingual vocab), license MIT (weights) · Apache-2.0 (runtime). Inference off the main thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: Honest about the download cost and mobile-memory implication; distinction from the English DistilBERT SQuAD demo stated up front and cross-linked.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: state 'download-required', single 'Download model (~295 MB)' action — NO silent auto-download of the large model. Warm reload in the same profile: state reached 'ready' automatically, the default answer re-rendered, and ZERO Download buttons present (verified programmatically: downloadBtnPresent=false). Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Critical for a 295 MB model: matches the auto-init contract exactly — absent → labelled Download, current cached → auto-init with no button, never a silent re-download.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live status region on every page; the shared loader announces checking→downloading→ready with a % progress bar during the ~295 MB download; a .status.err surfaces answering failures on the page; a low-confidence note fires when the start distribution peaks below ~0.3 (a model-grounded 'may be unanswerable' signal from the SQuAD2 head). Loader exposes Retry on failed init.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled native controls across the ladder: 8 language sample chips (min-block-size 2.2rem), dir=auto context textarea + question input, an Answer button, Enter-to-submit; practical builds per-sample question chips dynamically; multi-model adds two loaders + a document textarea + question chips. All keyboard-operable native elements.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass (focus order + visible focus on every chip and input at both viewports) is the remaining agent check; layout + operability verified.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the answer area stays hidden and the loader shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' Download state — nothing canned shown as live output. Every highlighted span comes from the on-device run (verified in-browser '330 metres tall' matched the Node span extraction).
rubric.notes: Confirmed no pre-baked result; sample text is preloaded but the answer only appears after a real run.

rubric.evidence: conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt (SVG data-URI favicon only). Answer spans render as semantic (not the CSS Custom Highlight API), so the highlight stays in the accessibility tree; the highlight container + answer + candidate rows carry dir=auto so Arabic renders RTL. A language badge labels the sample script.
rubric.notes: Followed highlight-text-ranges guidance: semantic <mark> for the meaningful answer span, not ::highlight() (which is only Baseline 2026 and 'AVOID as a replacement for semantic HTML').

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth, no clipped controls, console + network clean. Screenshots read: reports/responsive/screens/multilingual-qa/{desktop,mobile}.png — header/eyebrow/at-a-glance reflow cleanly, sample chips wrap, the token strip flex-wraps, textarea is full-width. support flipped to ok on both classes.
rubric.notes: The candidate grid uses minmax()+min-inline-size:0 and overflow-wrap:anywhere so long multilingual answers don't overflow on narrow viewports.

rubric.evidence: All QA inference (span scoring + the token-strip distributions) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) off the main thread; the multi-model rung runs the multilingual embedder in its own worker. Main-thread work (span re-location, render) is over a bounded token count.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; the O(n·span) candidate scan is bounded (MAX_SPAN=26) so it stays cheap even for long passages.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: For meaningful, labelled ranges prefer semantic HTML (<mark>) over the CSS Custom Highlight API, which is only Baseline 2026 and 'AVOID as a replacement for semantic HTML'.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the answer span renders as a semantic <mark class="qa-span"> via char offsets, not ::highlight() ranges, keeping the highlight in the accessibility tree; dir=auto handles RTL passages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: renderHighlighted() in mqa.js; in-browser run highlighted '330 metres tall' in the passage

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — QA inference (logits + the O(n·span) span search + the see-inside distributions) all runs in models/multilingual-qa/worker.js; the multi-model embedder runs in its own worker.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; first-visit run stayed responsive during the ~295 MB download

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + logical properties + minmax auto-fit grids and min-inline-size:0 to avoid horizontal overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chip rows and the token strip use flex-wrap; the candidate rows use a grid with minmax()+min-inline-size:0+overflow-wrap:anywhere; the multi-model loaders use grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px,1fr)); dir=auto handles RTL.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — answering failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the loader exposes a Retry path; a model-grounded low-confidence note warns when the answer may be unanswerable.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state; unanswerable-note

openQuestions: Drive a full keyboard traversal at mobile + desktop: is every language chip, the textarea, the question input and the Answer button reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, including for the RTL Arabic sample?
The ~295 MB q8 download is the heaviest QA demo; on a low-RAM phone does the WASM path OOM, and if so should the at-a-glance surface a device-memory pre-check before offering Download?
Cross-lingual QA (wild rung) has genuinely lower confidence than same-language QA — is the 0.25 peak threshold for the low-confidence note tuned right across languages, or should it adapt per script?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent keyboard-traversal + RTL focus pass for multilingual-qa across mobile+desktop, confirming focus order and visible focus on the Arabic sample.
Add a 'quiz any Wikipedia paragraph in your language' wild demo: paste a paragraph in any language and auto-generate a few sample questions to extract answers for, entirely on-device.

summary: multilingual-qa is a strong, honest, genuinely multilingual extractive-QA demo, distinct from the English DistilBERT SQuAD demo: one XLM-RoBERTa SQuAD2 model extracts answer spans over passages in ~100 languages, verified in Node across EN/DE/ES/FR/JA/IT and end-to-end in the browser (Download ~295 MB → ready → '330 metres tall'; warm-cache auto-init with no button; 0 console/network errors). The key correctness work is a whitespace-insensitive span re-location that fixes SentencePiece detokenisation so the multilingual answer is correctly spaced and highlightable as a semantic <mark>. Accurate at-a-glance (honest about the 295 MB cost), real start/end 'see inside', clean mobile+desktop layout (support ok), and a four-rung ladder (basics multi-language → practical multilingual FAQ → wild cross-lingual EN-question/FR-passage → multi-model multilingual-embed-retrieve-then-read). Revision 1.
